description: Foreign key constraints are ignored because the foreign_keys pragma is off by default, allowing orphaned rows.
body: Failure mode: the schema declares FOREIGN KEY constraints but foreign_keys is not enabled per connection, so SQLite parses but does not enforce them, letting inserts reference missing parents and deletes orphan children. Detection signals: rows referencing nonexistent parents; cascades not firing; foreign_keys pragma never set after open. Do not assume declared constraints are enforced. Safer fallback: set PRAGMA foreign_keys=ON on every connection, run PRAGMA foreign_key_check to find existing violations, and add a startup assertion that the pragma is on.
